text:
It was just after dawn. In a cozy little house on a narrow street lived a man named Harold. Harold loved birds. Every morning he would feed them. Chick chicks chirped joyously.

One morning Harold noticed a gray owl feather. It was delicate, unlike anything he'd ever found. Curious, Harold tucked it into his pocket.

The afternoon sun cast long shadows. Harold decided to take a walk. That's where he saw her—the snowy owl. It was beautiful. Almost lifesome. Harold couldn't bear the thought of her lost. He stooped down.

The owl eyed Harold warily. Birds didn't flutter about humans often.

"You lost something?" Harold said gently.

The owl hooted softly.

Harold reached into his pocket. There.

The owl blinked.

"Here."

The owl swooped down.

Harold threw the feather. It fluttered.

Then they soared.

That evening Harold sat on his porch. Stars peeked through.

The owl hooted
